bird.name: White-browed tit-spinetail
bird.summary: The white-browed tit-spinetail (Leptasthenura xenothorax) is a Near Threatened species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird…

relations.subject: White-browed tit-spinetail
relations.evidence: It primarily inhabits Polylepis woodlands but occasionally occurs in montane scrublands
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.subject: White-browed tit-spinetail
relations.evidence: Primary threats to the species include habitat degradation from fires and grazing. "High-Andean Polylepis forest ecosystems represent one of the most threatened woodland ecosystems in the world...which continues to decline in quantity and quality." On the brighter side, "[c]ommunity-based woodland habitat restoration efforts are currently underway at three locations in the Cordillera Vilcanota (Abra Malaga, Hulloc and Cancha-Cancha) under the guidance of the Peruvian non-governmental organization Asociación Ecosistemas Andinos (ECOAN)."
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
